title: garrytan/gstack
friendlyTitle: GStack is a ready-made AI product-building workflow
summary: GStack packages Garry Tan’s opinionated Claude Code setup into a workflow of skills for planning, design review, engineering review, QA, security review, release, documentation, and team memory. The repo frames the process as Think → Plan → Build → Review → Test → Ship → Reflect.
friendlySummary: GStack gives Claude Code a set of skills for planning, reviewing, testing, shipping, and learning from software work.
whyItMatters: Builders can use it as a ready-made operating system for AI-assisted product development, especially when they want structured reviews and guardrails around coding agents. It is also useful as a reference for designing multi-skill agent workflows.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Use it if you want a more organized way to work with coding agents instead of just asking one big prompt and hoping for the best.
